title: Piccle 1.0 document
description: Piccle v1.0 schema for finite procedural UI-audio synthesis documents. The normative DSP and conformance specification is published at https://dotpiccle.com.

$schema.description: Canonical Piccle v1 JSON Schema URI. Optional, but when present it must identify this schema.

piccle.description: The Piccle format version this document targets. '1.0' is the only valid value for v1.

name.description: Optional UTF-8 metadata label for the asset.

description.description: Optional UTF-8 metadata describing the asset's intended UI-audio function.

duration_ms.description: Total document duration in milliseconds. Optional — if absent, computed from the latest-ending layer. Shorter trims layers; longer pads with silence. With spatial effects, duration_ms plus each effect's tail extension must remain within the safe-integer bound.

master_volume_level.description: Final master gain applied to the spatial-effects result before clipping. 0 = silent, 1 = unity gain. A single number, unlike layer `volume` which accepts a contour object. Independent of per-layer volume.

spatial_effects.description: Optional whole-document spatial effects applied after the dry mix. Each entry is a reverb or echo. All effects run in parallel — each receives the same dry mix and adds its wet contribution. An empty array is valid and equivalent to omitting the field — no spatial effects are applied.

layers.description: One or more independently scheduled mono source paths, each with its own filter chain, linear-amplitude envelope, and equal-power stereo balance.

curves.description: Transition curve shapes used in volume, pitch, and filter frequency entries.

fade_stage.description: A boundary gain-interpolation stage defined by duration and interpolation curve. Used by fade_in and fade_out.

ms.description: Fade duration in milliseconds. 0 means no fade stage.

curve.description: Curve applied to the fade gain. Reuses the transition_curve enum.

filter.description: One serial second-order IIR section. Filter topology is fixed for the layer lifetime; cutoff or center frequency is a piecewise contour.

type.description: Second-order transfer-function class: lowpass, highpass, or constant-peak-gain bandpass.

frequencies.description: One or more cutoff-frequency targets evaluated from layer time zero.

hz.description: Cutoff frequency in Hz at this point. 20–20000 Hz range.

hold_ms.description: How long to hold at this cutoff before transitioning to the next, in milliseconds.

transition_ms.description: How long to move from this cutoff to the next, in milliseconds. Ignored on the last entry.

transition_curve.description: Shape of the transition from this cutoff to the next. Ignored on the last entry.

resonance.description: Normalized resonance control mapped linearly to Q over approximately [0.707, 12].

layer.description: An independently scheduled mono source path with optional serial biquads, a linear-amplitude envelope, and equal-power stereo panning.

id.description: Unique identifier for this layer. Lowercase letters, digits, and hyphens only.

start_ms.description: When this layer starts, in milliseconds from the document start. Defaults to 0. Layers that omit start_ms all start at the document start simultaneously; the order of layers in the layers array does not affect timing.

duration_ms.description: How long this layer plays, in milliseconds. start_ms plus duration_ms must remain within the safe-integer bound.

source.description: Mono excitation generator: a band-limited periodic oscillator or deterministic noise source.

volume.description: Linear-amplitude envelope. A scalar in [0, 1] selects constant base gain with terminal fade; an object defines fade stages and piecewise level targets.

balance.description: Equal-power mono-to-stereo pan position: -1 = left, 0 = center, 1 = right.

filters.description: Serial filter chain in array order; each entry receives the preceding entry's output. The schema imposes no maximum, and each Piccle engine profile publishes any enforced filter-count limit.

pitch.description: Fundamental-frequency control for a tone source: a piecewise frequency contour plus optional equal-tempered cents offset.

frequencies.description: One or more fundamental-frequency targets evaluated from layer time zero.

hz.description: Fundamental-frequency target in Hz at this contour point; range [20, 20000].

hold_ms.description: How long to hold at this pitch before transitioning to the next, in milliseconds.

transition_ms.description: How long to move from this pitch to the next, in milliseconds. Ignored on the last entry.

transition_curve.description: Shape of the transition from this pitch to the next. Ignored on the last entry.

offset_cents.description: Pitch offset in cents (100 cents = 1 semitone). Applied after contour interpolation and before render-profile frequency clamping.

spatial_effect.description: One parallel document-level wet-processing branch selected by a type discriminator. Each branch receives the shared dry stereo mix.

type.description: Spatial-processing topology: diffused FDN reverb or lowpass-feedback comb echo.

spatial_reverb.description: Parallel diffused eight-line FDN reverb branch with additive wet gain, RT60/tail control, and wet-path lowpass. All fields are required.

type.description: Discriminator identifying this entry as a reverb effect.

amount.description: Additive wet gain. 0 = no reverb contribution, 1 = reverb at full level. Dry is always present from the parallel spatial-effects stage.

tail_ms.description: Reverb RT60 target and emitted wet-tail duration, in milliseconds.

soften_hz.description: Corner frequency of the normative first-order wet-path lowpass, in Hz.

spatial_echo.description: Parallel two-channel lowpass-feedback comb branch. delay_ms defines delay-line length, feedback controls recursive decay, wet_gain scales the additive wet contribution, and damp_hz sets the feedback-path lowpass corner. All fields are required.

type.description: Discriminator identifying this entry as an echo effect.

delay_ms.description: Time between successive echoes in milliseconds, and the time to the first echo. 1 or more.

feedback.description: Feedback coefficient in [0, 1). Zero produces one repeat; values approaching unity increase recursive decay time and the derived tail. Values that exceed the bounded tail-length calculation are invalid.

wet_gain.description: Linear gain applied to the echo wet contribution before parallel summation with the unity dry path; range [0, 1].

damp_hz.description: Corner frequency, in Hz, of the first-order lowpass in the echo feedback path; range [200, 12000].

source.description: Closed union of mono excitation generators: a band-limited periodic oscillator or deterministic RMS-normalized noise source.

type.description: Selects the deterministic band-limited periodic oscillator source.

wave.description: Periodic waveform class: sinusoid; odd-harmonic triangle with 1/k² rolloff; odd-harmonic square with 1/k rolloff; or all-harmonic saw with 1/k rolloff.

pitch.description: Fundamental-frequency contour and optional equal-tempered cents offset for the oscillator.

type.description: Selects the deterministic PCG32 noise excitation source.

character.description: Noise transfer characteristic: 400 Hz first-order lowpass (soft), identity (neutral), or 2 kHz first-order highpass (sharp).

seed.description: Unsigned 32-bit seed for the normative PCG32 noise stream. Equal character and seed values reproduce the same raw stream.

volume.description: Post-filter linear-amplitude envelope. A scalar is shorthand for constant base gain plus terminal fade; an object defines piecewise targets and independently curved boundary fades.

oneOf.description: Constant linear-amplitude gain with a terminal linear fade over min(5 ms, layer duration).

fade_in.description: Boundary interpolation from zero to the first level target; default 0 ms linear.

fade_out.description: Terminal interpolation from the held final level to zero; default 5 ms linear.

levels.description: One or more linear-amplitude targets for the layer volume contour.

level.description: Linear-amplitude target at this contour point; range [0, 1].

hold_ms.description: How long to hold at this level before transitioning to the next, in milliseconds.

transition_ms.description: How long to move from this level to the next, in milliseconds. Ignored on the last entry.

transition_curve.description: Shape of the transition from this level to the next. Ignored on the last entry.
